canonicalization_rules: Tool outputs compare stable text content and structured details after ignoring elapsed timing, process ids, temporary directory roots, and filesystem-specific absolute paths.
Bash stdout and stderr are treated as a merged tool output stream because both become model-visible tool-result text in pi-mono and Rust Pi.
Bash timeout parity is fail-closed: timeout must mark the tool result as an error, include the timeout message, and prevent descendant process output after SIGTERM-to-kill escalation.
SSE chunk boundaries are not semantically meaningful; parser tests compare parsed event type, data, id, and retry fields across CRLF, split event, multiline data, and carried id/retry cases.
Truncation parity is validated through structured truncation metadata rather than raw temporary full-output paths.

coverage: atomic write with parent directory creation

acceptance_evidence.criterion: tests/dropin_tool_io_differential/ with at least 30 tool scenarios passing
acceptance_evidence.evidence: 41 executable scenarios defined in tests/dropin_tool_io_differential/g09_tool_io_scenarios.json and run by tests/dropin_tool_io_differential.rs

acceptance_evidence.criterion: Streaming chunk test passing with explicit canonicalization rules
acceptance_evidence.evidence: SSE scenarios cover CRLF, split event, multiline data, id/retry carry-forward, and empty event-name defaulting; canonicalization rules are recorded in this artifact.

acceptance_evidence.criterion: Bash process-tree kill behavior matches pi-mono on SIGTERM-to-SIGKILL escalation
acceptance_evidence.evidence: bash.timeout.process_group starts a descendant writer, times out the process group, waits beyond the descendant delay, and verifies no leaked file is written.
docs/evidence/dropin-certification-verdict.json marks G09-integration-io-parity as pass and indexes this artifact.
